title: Fragile electron-phonon superconductivity in MnB4 under pressure
authors: Renhai Wang, Shiya Chen, Feng Zheng, Zhen Zhang, Xingzhi Wang, Huafeng Dong, Cai-Zhuang Wang, Vladimir Antropov, Kai-Ming Ho, Yang Sun
abstract: The origin of pressure-induced superconductivity in MnB4 remains unclear. Here we show that it can be explained by electron-phonon coupling once the structural space is mapped using both volume and the Mn dimer distance as key structural parameters under compression. Minor changes in the dimer distance significantly affect electronic and phonon properties, bringing the calculated Tc into agreement with experiment. Our results suggest that MnB4 is a highly responsive system, providing a platform for probing the subtle interplay between structural instability, superconductivity and magnetism.
summary: This work resolves the mystery of pressure-induced superconductivity in $	ext{MnB}_4$ by demonstrating that structural compression must be described using two parameters: volume and the Mn-Mn dimer distance. By calculating electron-phonon coupling across this extended structural space, the authors successfully predict the experimentally observed critical temperature via established BCS/EPC theory.
main_problem: The primary goal is to elucidate the mechanism behind pressure-induced superconductivity in $	ext{MnB}_4$, as initial calculations failed to match experimental critical temperatures ($T_c$). The study investigates how structural changes influence electron-phonon coupling (EPC) in this material.
main_result: Superconductivity can be successfully explained by EPC theory when the structural response is mapped using both volume and the $	ext{Mn}-	ext{Mn}$ dimer distance as independent parameters. Calculations predict $T_c \approx 14	ext{ K}$ near $150	ext{ GPa}$, matching experimental findings.
method: The research combines Density Functional Theory (DFT) calculations with advanced phonon theory to compute EPC strength ($\lambda$) and estimate $T_c$ using the Allen-Dynes formula. Structural mapping is achieved by analyzing the energy landscape in the volume-$	ext{d}_{	ext{Mn}-	ext{Mn}}$ space.
model_or_system: The system studied is $	ext{MnB}_4$, which is treated as a highly responsive material exhibiting structural instability characterized by Mn-Mn dimerization. The theoretical framework relies on modeling the interplay between lattice structure, electronic band filling, and vibrational modes (phonons).
key_observables: Superconducting critical temperature ($T_c$), electron-phonon coupling constant ($\lambda_{\Gamma}$), $	ext{Mn}-	ext{Mn}$ dimer distance ($	ext{d}_{	ext{Mn}-	ext{Mn}}$), and electronic density of states at the Fermi level ($N(E_F)$).
important_parameters: Pressure (P) up to $150	ext{ GPa}$, Volume ($V$), and $	ext{d}_{	ext{Mn}-	ext{Mn}}$.
main_assumptions: The superconducting behavior is governed by the electron-phonon coupling mechanism, allowing for the use of the Allen-Dynes formula. The structural response is assumed to be tunable across a defined $V-	ext{d}_{	ext{Mn}-	ext{Mn}}$ manifold.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the comparison between DFT and experimental XRD data mapping the $V-	ext{d}_{	ext{Mn}-	ext{Mn}}$ energy landscape. They also show how $\lambda_{\Gamma}$ and $T_c$ increase rapidly with increasing $	ext{d}_{	ext{Mn}-	ext{Mn}}$ at fixed volume, confirming the role of dimer stretching modes.
paper_structure: The paper first establishes the failure of simple volume-based models. It then introduces $	ext{d}_{	ext{Mn}-	ext{Mn}}$ as a crucial structural parameter, mapping the full energy landscape. Subsequently, it calculates EPC parameters ($\lambda_{\Gamma}$) across this space, demonstrating that the observed $T_c$ is robustly explained by strong coupling mechanisms linked to dimer bond changes.
why_interesting: While focused on condensed matter superconductivity, the methodology of treating structural instability via multiple coupled degrees of freedom ($V$ and $	ext{d}_{	ext{Mn}-	ext{Mn}}$) provides a generalizable framework for understanding phase transitions in complex materials where simple single-parameter descriptions fail.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1 Mn-Mn dimerization and two-parameter energy landscape in MnB4. (a) P6/mmm MgB2 structure with regular B hexagons and a uniform Mg-Mg chain with a distance of 3.1Å. (b) Orthorhombic Pnnm FeB4 structure with Fe atoms form an undimerized one-dimensional metal chain with equal Fe-Fe separations of ∼3.0Å. Crossed and uncrossed Fe atoms denote two layers occupying distinct interstitial positions between neighboring boron layers. (c) Monoclinic P21/c MnB4 structure with Mn chain dimerized into alternating short and long Mn-Mn bonds, with representative distances of ∼2.7 and ∼3.2Å, respectively. (d) Representative refinements of the experimental XRD pattern at 110 GPa using the MnB4...

FIG. 2 Electron-phonon coupling and superconductivity landscape in MnB4. (a) λΓ in the V–dMn-Mn plane, constructed from 561 sampled structures. The blue curve denotes the DFT relaxed structure with the shaded region indicating the dMn-Mn variation caused by zero-point motion. The red curve denotes the experimentally refined structural path, with the shaded region indicating the uncertainty in dMn-Mn from XRD refinement. (b) Full-Brillouin-zone Tc calculations for representative structures selected from panel (a), plotted as a function of λΓ. The solid line gives the linear fitting with R2=0.983. (c) Tc and λΓ as functions of dMn-Mnat V=5.89 Å3/atom, corresponding to 110 GPa in experiments....

Figure 1(e) also compares the V-dMn-Mn relation obtained from DFT optimization with that from experimentally refined structures. At the ambient-pressure volume of 7.355 Å3/atom, the equilibrium dMn-Mn from DFT differs from the experimental value by only ~0.15 Å, and the confidence intervals overlap. Under compression, however, this offset increases: at ~5.7 Å3/atom, corresponding to ~140 GPa in experiment, the difference reaches ~0.3 Å. Thus, DFT calculations can yield dMn-Mn values that differ from experimentally refined structures by 0.1-0.3 Å, with the deviation tending to increase under compression, even after accounting for the uncertainty in dMn-Mn from both approaches.

FIG. 3 Experimental and calculated pressure dependence of Tc in MnB4. (a) Experimental Tc values from Ref. [4] are shown in red. The upper axis shows the pressure corresponding to the experimental equation of state. The green curve gives the calculated Tc obtained from the Tc–V– dMn-Mn map in Fig. 2(d). The green bar and shaded region represent the range of Tc values associated with the experimental uncertainty in dMn-Mn. (b) Phonon dispersion of MnB4 at dMn-Mn = 2.548 Å and V = 5.89 Å3/atom, corresponding to 110 GPa in experiment. The dispersion is shown together with the momentum- and mode-resolved phonon linewidth γqν, phonon density of states, Eliashberg spectral function α2F(ω) , and...

FIG. 4 Magnetic and electronic properties in the V–dMn-Mn space. Calculated maps of (a) the Mn magnetic moment and (b) the density of states at the Fermi level N(ϵF) of MnB4 as functions of V and dMn-Mn. The blue and red curves denote the static 0 K DFT relaxed structure and the experimental structural parameters, respectively.
